channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Rubbish dumped by side of dustbins, 15th March
item.description:
Unsightly rubbish has been left by bins. It has been here for a few weeks. All along the road and pavements there are dead leaves and litter

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Webb Road, Blackheath
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: White gravel on public realm border of Deal Porters Walk, 15th March
item.description:
A house holder on Hythe Point has 'captured' part of the planted border on Deal Porters Walk to make a path from the gate they have installed in their fence. The path is very unsightly - made of white gravel - and could unfairly encourage others to do the same. I think the path has been made in the last few days and looks like a continuation of the white gravel in their garden.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Deal Porters Walk, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Oil or diesel spill on pavement creating slip hazard, 15th March
item.description:
There appears to be a significant oil or diesel spill along the pavement and kerb on this street. The substance has spread across multiple paving slabs and continues along a stretch of the pavement, as shown in the photos. The surface looks greasy and could present a slip hazard for pedestrians, particularly in wet conditions. It is also causing visible staining to the paving. This may require specialist street cleansing, such as high-pressure hot water jet washing or degreasing treatment, to remove the contamination and restore the surface. The spill appears to extend along a section of the pavement rather than being a small isolated patch. Outside 155 Old Brompton Road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 157, Old Brompton Road, South Kensington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Graham Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 15th March
item.description:
On 14/3/2026 at 1600hrs whilst driving through the golf course road to gain entry to orchard allotment we saw a white ford transit van parked opposite the gate entrance to the allotment. Inside the can were 2 males, eastern European looking. One was 30s and the other 50/60s. On our exit at 1705hrs the van was still parked in the same location. My wife took a photo of the van, however, the number plate is very blurred and not readable. The following day at 1015hrs i returned to the allotment and noticed a pile of rubbish at the location where the van had been parked.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mayplace Road East, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned untaxed and No M.O.T car, 15th March
item.description:
This car has been abandoned at this small private car park for several months now, the people who i believe owned this car have now moved from Sandway Road and have left this vehicle behind. It has no tax and no MOT. It is in a small car park opposite house number 5 Sandway Road, Orpington, Kent BR5 3TU

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 7, Sandway Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Huge pile of litter dumped on the road outside De Beauvoir Crescent 42-44, 15th March
item.description:
I found a letter with a name in the pile (see attached Amazon order confirmation. This might give you a hint as to who was the perpetrator.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): De Beauvoir Crescent, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
